solution.p: To add or subtract two fractions with different denominators, we first have to find a common denominator.
In this case, the least common multiple of 10 and 6 is 30.
Look at the first fraction \displaystyle{-\frac{3}{10}}. To change the denominator to 30, we multiply 3 at both the top and bottom, and we have:
Similarly, we change the denominator of the second fraction to 30 by:
Finally, we add or subtract the numerators and keep the common denominator unchanged. The whole process is:
